patterns: Can I reduce fire risks at home?
Help reduce fire risks at home right now
reduce fire risks home
can i reduce fire risks at home
how reduce fire risks at home pls
responses: Declutter, maintain wiring, keep heat sources clear, and store fuels safely. Create a checklist and review monthly. Never use water on grease or electrical fires.

patterns: What is the best way to respond when a small fire starts at night?
Help respond when a small fire starts at night!!!
respond when small fire starts night
what is the best way 2 respond when a small fire starts at night
how respond when a small fire starts at night pls
responses: Alert everyone, evacuate along your plan, call 911/BFP, and only attempt a quick extinguisher use if trained and the exit is behind you. Check smoke alarms monthly and replace batteries yearly.

patterns: How do I get a fire safety inspection certificate?
Help get a fire safety inspection certificate pls help!
get fire safety inspection certificate
how do i get a fire safety inspect cert
how get a fire safety inspection certificate pls
responses: Prepare building plans, extinguisher records, and electrical clearances, then apply at the BFP office. Schedule inspection and address any noted findings. Check smoke alarms monthly and replace batteries yearly.

patterns: What is the best way to treat a minor burn safely?
ASAP: treat a minor burn safely right now
treat minor burn safely
what is the best way 2 treat a minor burn safely
how treat a minor burn safely pls
responses: Cool under running water for 20 minutes, remove tight items, cover with a clean non-stick dressing, and seek care if blistered or large. Do not apply butter or toothpaste. Never use water on grease or electrical fires.

patterns: Why does teach students about fire safety?
Emergency! why does teach students about fire safety pls help!
why does teach students about fire safety
why does teach students about fire safety pls
responses: Use age-appropriate activities, practice evacuation, assign marshals, and brief on stop, drop, and roll. Coordinate drills with the local fire station. Teach children stop, drop, and roll and how to call for help.
